extension.valueMarkdown:
## Character
6-year-old Indian-American girl. Lives with her mother Priya Patel (age 34, software engineer) and father Raj Patel (age 36, pharmacist) in Raleigh, NC. Attends first grade. Generally active and happy but has significant allergic disease burden.

## Clinical Scenario
Aisha was diagnosed with asthma at age 3 after recurrent wheezing with viral URIs. She progressed to severe persistent asthma by age 5, requiring daily fluticasone/salmeterol plus montelukast, with PRN albuterol. She has confirmed IgE-mediated food allergies to peanuts (anaphylaxis at age 2 — epinephrine auto-injector prescribed) and tree nuts (positive skin prick and specific IgE testing). She also has moderate atopic dermatitis managed with topical steroids and moisturizers, and allergic rhinitis.

Her care involves a pediatrician for routine well-child visits and acute illness, a pediatric allergist/immunologist for allergy management and immunotherapy evaluation, and she has had two ED visits at a children's hospital — one for an anaphylactic reaction at age 2 (accidental peanut exposure at a family gathering) and one for a severe asthma exacerbation at age 5 requiring systemic steroids.

Priya Patel, as Aisha's mother, is the authorized representative who uses a patient-facing app to manage Aisha's health records.

## Sites
- **Triangle Pediatrics** — Pediatrician in Raleigh, NC (2020–present). Well-child visits, immunizations, acute visits, asthma management.
- **UNC Pediatric Allergy & Immunology** — Allergist in Chapel Hill, NC (2022–present). Food allergy testing, asthma specialist management, immunotherapy evaluation.
- **WakeMed Children's Emergency Department** — ED in Raleigh, NC. Two visits: anaphylaxis (2022) and severe asthma exacerbation (2025).

Three sites, one state (NC). Tests Permission Ticket constraints primarily on **scopes** (e.g., can filter to just immunization records, or just allergy-related data) and **organizations** (3 different health systems).

code.text: IgE-mediated peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

note.text: Peanut-specific IgE remains very high at 41.6 kU/L (Class 5, slight decline from 45.3). Supervised oral food challenge not appropriate at this level. Peanut oral immunotherapy (Palforzia protocol) discussed in detail with family. Decision pending.
Specific IgE values trending down but still significantly positive (cashew 14.8, walnut 9.7, almond 4.9 kU/L). Continue strict avoidance. No change in management.

Upgraded from moderate persistent to severe persistent asthma. Improved on Advair HFA 115/21 BID plus montelukast. FeNO improved from 38 to 24 ppb. Spirometry shows mild obstruction with borderline bronchodilator response. Rescue inhaler use approximately twice weekly. Omalizumab discussed as potential next step once patient turns 6 in March 2026.
Almond-specific IgE 8.3 kU/L. SPT 5 mm wheal / 16 mm flare (positive). No known ingestion reaction. Restricted given cross-reactivity risk and young age.

manifestation.text: No known ingestion reaction; positive sensitization on testing

note.text: Cashew nut-specific IgE 22.7 kU/L. SPT 9 mm wheal / 28 mm flare (positive). No known ingestion reaction. Restricted given cross-reactivity risk and young age.

manifestation.text: No known ingestion reaction; positive sensitization on testing

note.text: Peanut-specific IgE 48.2 kU/L (Class 5, very high). SPT 12 mm wheal / 42 mm flare (strongly positive). Anaphylaxis confirmed by elevated tryptase (18.4 ng/mL) at WakeMed ED, October 2022. Strict avoidance required. EpiPen Jr prescribed.
Walnut-specific IgE 15.1 kU/L. SPT 7 mm wheal / 22 mm flare (positive). No known ingestion reaction. Restricted given cross-reactivity risk and young age.

coding.display: Fractional concentration of exhaled nitric oxide

resource.conclusion: FeNO 38 ppb — elevated for age (normal <20 ppb). Consistent with eosinophilic airway inflammation despite current medium-dose ICS/LABA plus LTRA therapy.

Spirometry was attempted but was technically inadequate. Patient (age 4 years 2 months) was unable to perform a reproducible forced expiratory maneuver. Could not sustain exhalation for the minimum acceptable duration. Flow-volume loops were not reproducible across 6 attempts. Test documented as not interpretable. Will be reattempted at next visit when patient is older and more cooperative.

coding.display: CBC W Auto Differential panel - Blood

resource.conclusion: CBC with differential unremarkable except for mild eosinophilia (6%, 480/uL) consistent with known atopic disease. WBC, hemoglobin, and platelets within normal limits.

All specific IgE values show modest downward trends. Peanut-specific IgE remains very high at 45.3 kU/L (Class 5). Tree nut specific IgE values continue slow decline but remain Class 3. Total IgE remains elevated for age.

coding.display: Fractional concentration of exhaled nitric oxide

resource.conclusion: FeNO 24 ppb — mildly elevated for age (normal <20 ppb). Improved from 38 ppb in May 2024, suggesting improved eosinophilic airway inflammation control on current higher-dose ICS/LABA regimen.

All specific IgE values show continued downward trends. Peanut-specific IgE remains very high at 41.6 kU/L (Class 5, down from 45.3). Tree nut specific IgE values continue slow decline but remain Class 3. Total IgE 356 IU/mL, still markedly elevated but declining.

code.text: Spirometry with pre- and post-bronchodilator testing

resource.conclusion: Spirometry performed successfully. FEV1 1.02 L (92% predicted), FVC 1.18 L (96% predicted), FEV1/FVC 0.86 (normal). Post-bronchodilator FEV1 1.12 L (10% improvement — borderline positive, threshold 12%). Mild obstruction with partial reversibility, consistent with asthma.

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Krishnamurthy called Priya following Dr.

Aisha, age 5 years 6 months, was seen via telemedicine for routine asthma and allergy follow-up. Priya reported that asthma control had been generally good through the summer on Advair HFA 115/21 BID, montelukast 4 mg daily, and cetirizine 5 mg daily.

coding.display: Office visit for pediatric care and assessment (procedure)

extension.valueMarkdown: Aisha, age 5 years 11 months, was seen for a comprehensive in-person allergy and asthma review. Priya reported that since the telemedicine visit in September 2025, asthma control had been stable.
